description:
As a security engineer, you need to automate the monthly rotation of credentials by scheduling a cron job and leaving an auditable trail of what you did.

    Complete ALL of the following steps from the Linux terminal (the home directory is /home/user).  Use any valid sequence of commands, but everything you create must exactly match the specifications below because automated grading will look for these precise artefacts.

    1. Create the directory:
       /home/user/security

    2. Inside that directory create an executable shell script called:
       /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh

       The file MUST contain the following three non-comment, non-blank lines in the exact order shown (the first line is the she-bang).  Do not include extra spaces or trailing whitespace:

       #!/bin/bash  
       # Rotate application credentials and keep an audit trail  
       echo "[\$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] Credentials rotated." >> /home/user/security/rotate_creds.log  

       Make sure the script is given executable permissions.

    3. Add the following cron entry to YOUR user’s crontab:

       45 2 1 * * /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh

       (This runs at 02:45 on the 1st day of every month.)

    4. Immediately after installing the new cron entry, export the full contents of the resulting crontab to this plain-text file:
       /home/user/security/final_crontab.txt

       The exported file must include the exact cron line shown in step 3 (it can include other pre-existing lines as well).

    5. Create the setup log file:
       /home/user/security/rotation_setup.log

       It must contain *exactly two lines* in the following format (replace the timestamp with the actual date and time when you run the command):

       Line 1:  Setup Timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
       Line 2:  Cron job 45 2 1 * * /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh installed  

       • “Setup Timestamp:” must start with an uppercase “S”.  
       • The timestamp uses the 24-hour clock and must match %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.  
       • There is a single space after each colon.  
       • Line 2 must be *exactly* as shown, including single spaces between every field of the cron expression.

    Verification checklist used by the grader:
      a) /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh exists, is executable, and contains the three required lines in order.  
      b) /home/user/security/final_crontab.txt exists and contains the cron expression “45 2 1 * * /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh”.  
      c) /home/user/security/rotation_setup.log exists and its second line is exactly “Cron job 45 2 1 * * /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh installed”.  

    NOTE: The cron daemon itself does not need to run during grading; only the files and their contents are checked.  Do NOT hard-code the timestamp in rotation_setup.log; generate it dynamically at runtime.
truth:
Directory that must be present:
      /home/user/security

    File: /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh  
    Exact content (3 lines, with a trailing newline after each line):
    1: #!/bin/bash\n
    2: # Rotate application credentials and keep an audit trail\n
    3: echo "[\$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] Credentials rotated." >> /home/user/security/rotate_creds.log\n

    Permissions: executable by the user (mode 755 or 744 both acceptable to grader).

    Crontab entry that must exist in the user’s crontab AND appear verbatim inside:
      /home/user/security/final_crontab.txt
        45 2 1 * * /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh

    Log file: /home/user/security/rotation_setup.log  
    The grader only asserts that this file exists, has at least two lines, and that the SECOND line is exactly:
       Cron job 45 2 1 * * /home/user/security/rotate_creds.sh installed

    The first line can be anything that matches the pattern:
       Setup Timestamp: <20-character timestamp in %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S format>

    No other files or directories are evaluated.
